capability_objective: From the long shipment fixture, resolve “Parcel Driftwood” in Portland, status active. Start with a scoped search; do not guess an ID from the surrounding records. After search identifies the record, fetch its full details by that returned ID. Ignore same-name records in other locations and slightly different names in Portland. Make no persistent changes. Finish with one sentence beginning “Found:” and include the stable ID.
prompt:
Resolve one shipment for the logistics exception desk in this sandbox. Read `SHIPMENTS.md`, then use the genuine local `./shipmentctl` executable through the harness's shell tool for every shipment-data lookup. Do not inspect the executable source, `.protected/`, `.shipment-runtime/`, the execution journal, the tool-managed receipt, or any backing store. Do not write a script, function, loop, wrapper, or compound command to perform the lookup workflow.

Find the shipment named “Parcel Driftwood” in Portland whose status is active. Your first shipment-data assistant action must be one exact, scoped search using all three requested constraints. Do not broaden the search and do not guess a stable ID from neighboring or similarly shaped records. Wait for that search response. Only if it contains exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID, use that returned ID for one full-record retrieval in the immediately following shipment-data assistant action. The search and retrieval must be separate direct executable invocations in separate shell-tool actions. Ignore same-name records in other locations, the exact name with another status, and slightly different names in Portland. Treat the successful full-record retrieval—not the search summary—as the source for the final name, location, status, and stable ID.

This is read-only. Do not list the collection, read preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, notify, or change shipment state. Do not invoke any shipment operation beyond the required scoped search and its conditional retrieval. Apart from `answer.txt` and audit artifacts written automatically by the executable, create or modify nothing. Do not expose notes, carrier details, dates, service levels, unrelated matches, or any other extra record fields.

Write the grounded result to `answer.txt` as exactly one sentence and use that identical sentence as your entire final response. Use this format:

```text
Found: <NAME> in <LOCATION> is <STATUS> with stable ID `<STABLE_ID>`.
```

Replace every placeholder from the retrieved full record, preserve its spelling and case, include the shown backticks around the ID, and include one trailing newline in the file. Run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` after writing the file.
